hero.title: 3 Days in Varanasi.
hero.titleAccent: The Oldest Living City and the Sacred Ganges.
hero.intro: There is nowhere on earth like Varanasi. One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities anywhere, where life and death play out openly on the ghats of the Ganges. The dawn boat ride and the evening Ganga Aarti will stay with you forever.

breadcrumb.label: 3 Days Varanasi: Sunrise, Sunset & Ganga Aarti

hero.heroImageAlt: Ganga Aarti ceremony Varanasi ghats at dusk

overview.heading: 3 Days Varanasi: Sunrise, Sunset & Ganga Aarti: The Full Picture
body: Varanasi, also called Kashi or Banaras, is the spiritual capital of India and one of the oldest living cities in the world. The ghats that line the Ganges are the stage for a daily cycle of prayer, bathing, cremation and celebration that has continued unbroken for thousands of years. It is intense, beautiful and unforgettable.

This 3 day Varanasi tour package from Malaysia centres on the two great experiences: the sunrise boat ride along the ghats as the city wakes, and the spectacular Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat at dusk, with fire, bells and chanting. A 2-night stay gives you time to absorb it all, with optional Pind Daan prayers if you wish.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat
items.body: Each evening, priests perform a choreographed ceremony of fire, incense and chanting on the main ghat. Watched from a boat on the river or from the steps, it is one of the most powerful spiritual spectacles in India.

items.title: Sunrise Boat Ride on the Ganges
items.body: Drift along the ghats at first light as pilgrims bathe, sadhus pray and the rising sun turns the old city gold. The quiet dawn boat ride is the most magical hour in Varanasi.

items.title: Temples, Ghats & Sarnath
items.body: Visit Kashi Vishwanath, one of the holiest Shiva temples, walk the labyrinth of old lanes, and drive out to Sarnath where the Buddha gave his first sermon.

days.title: Arrive Varanasi
days.description: Met at Lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport and transferred to your hotel. In the evening, witness the Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat, ideally from a boat on the river.
suggestedActivities: Evening Ganga Aarti from a boat

days.title: Sunrise Boat Ride, Temples & Sarnath
days.description: An early sunrise boat ride along the ghats, then temples and the Buddhist site of Sarnath with your private vehicle.

days.title: Free Morning, Depart
days.description: Checkout at noon. Time for Banarasi silk shopping or a final ghat visit before your airport transfer.
suggestedActivities: Lassi at a famous old shop

whyBook.heading: Varanasi, With the Right Guidance
whyBook.body: Varanasi can overwhelm first-timers. A knowledgeable guide and well-timed boat rides turn the intensity into something profound rather than confusing. We handle the boats, the timings and the lanes. Dhesu has arranged spiritual India journeys since 1988.

sections.heading: Varanasi Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
Varanasi is unlike anywhere else, and a guided private trip is the best way to experience it. The dawn boat ride and the evening Ganga Aarti are the headline experiences, and timing them well makes all the difference.

Our driver guide knows the ghats, the temple lanes and the river, so you see the city's depth without getting lost in its intensity.

Varanasi pairs naturally with Ayodhya and Prayagraj for a deeper spiritual circuit, covered in our longer North India packages.

sections.heading: The Ghats of Varanasi
sections.body:
More than 80 ghats line the Ganges in Varanasi, each with its own character. Dashashwamedh is the main ceremonial ghat where the evening aarti takes place. Manikarnika and Harishchandra are the cremation ghats, where the cycle of life and death is laid bare.

The dawn boat ride lets you take in the whole sweep of them as the city comes to life, the single most memorable hour of most visits.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport, Varanasi (VNS). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Varanasi?
items.answer: October to March offers the most comfortable weather for early boat rides and ghat walks. Dev Deepawali (November) is spectacular but very crowded. Summers are extremely hot, and the monsoon raises the river level, sometimes limiting ghat access.

items.question: Is the sunrise boat ride included?
items.answer: Yes, this package includes both the sunrise boat ride along the ghats and an evening boat for the Ganga Aarti, which are the two essential Varanasi experiences.

items.question: Can I arrange Pind Daan or special prayers?
items.answer: Yes. Many visitors come to Varanasi for Pind Daan and ancestral rites. We can arrange these with experienced pandits as part of your trip. Please let us know when you book so we can prepare.
